# Breast Cancer Screening Free Under Virginia Law — But Bills Keep Coming

**TL;DR:** A Virginia law requires free breast cancer screenings, but some Chesterfield residents are still receiving unexpected bills.

Virginia law requires health insurance plans to cover breast cancer screenings with no out-of-pocket costs to patients. Despite this requirement, some residents across the state—including in Chesterfield—continue to receive bills for these screenings, indicating a gap between the law's intent and its implementation. Patients who receive unexpected bills should review their statements and understand their right to dispute charges for covered screenings.

**Why it matters:** Chesterfield residents should know their coverage rights and take action if they're billed for breast cancer screenings, which should be provided at no cost under state law.
